user_request: Draft a civil suit for recovery of Rs.10,00,000/- where the Defendant issued a cheque dated 15.01.2025 for Rs.10,00,000/- drawn on State Bank of India which was dishonoured on presentation with endorsement 'Insufficient Funds'. A legal notice under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act was sent on 01.02.2025 but the Defendant failed to pay within 15 days. Plead under Section 138 NI Act and also claim civil recovery with interest at 18% per annum. Include cause of action, limitation, valuation, court fee and verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

facts.summary: Recovery suit for Rs. 10,00,000/- based on a cheque dated 15.01.2025 issued by the Defendant on State Bank of India, which was dishonoured due to insufficient funds. A legal notice under Section 138 NI Act was sent on 01.02.2025, and payment was not made within the stipulated 15 days.

chronology.event: Cheque for Rs. 10,00,000/- issued by Defendant drawn on State Bank of India
Cheque presented for negotiation and dishonoured with endorsement 'Insufficient Funds'
Legal notice under Section 138 NI Act sent to Defendant
Defendant failed to pay within 15 days of notice

slots.value: Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act
Cause of action, limitation, valuation, court fee, verification clause

missing_fields: Plaintiff and Defendant names and addresses
Details of legal notice dispatch (e.g., courier/post)
assumptions: Suit is filed after expiry of 15-day notice period
Jurisdiction lies with the specified District Court based on cause of action or residence

sections.body:
SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- (Rupees {{CLAIM_AMOUNT_WORDS}} Only) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS
(Filed under Section 26 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908)

sections.heading: BETWEEN
sections.instruction: Draft the parties section listing plaintiff and defendant(s) with full particulars: name, age, occupation, and address. Use sui juris averment for competency. If any detail is missing, use the appropriate {{PLACEHOLDER}}.

sections.heading: JURISDICTION
sections.instruction: Draft the jurisdiction section establishing territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction. For territorial jurisdiction, state SPECIFIC facts: where the Defendant resides or carries on business, where payment was made, where the agreement was executed, or where the cause of action arose — with concrete place references. For pecuniary jurisdiction, state the claim amount and that it falls within this court's monetary limits.

sections.heading: FACTS OF THE CASE
sections.instruction: Draft the facts section as numbered paragraphs in chronological narrative form. Each paragraph must tell a story — not state conclusions. Cover: (1) How the parties came to know each other and the nature of their relationship, (2) What was agreed — the specific terms of the transaction/agreement, (3) What obligations each party undertook and the timeline for performance, (4) How payment was made — specific amount, mode, date, (5) What exactly constituted the defendant's failure — describe the specific default with detail, (6) What happened after the default — communications, demands, legal notice and response. Reference documents as 'Annexure [LABEL]'. Do NOT state legal conclusions in this section — keep it purely factual.

sections.heading: LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS
sections.instruction: Draft the legal basis section in paragraph form. For each statutory provision: (1) State what the provision says — the legal rule it creates, (2) Explain what factual conditions trigger its application, (3) Show how the facts of this case satisfy those conditions. If applicable, include an alternative plea (e.g., Section 73 ICA as alternative to Section 65 ICA) using 'In the alternative and without prejudice to the above...' framing. Include defensive pleading: pre-emptively neutralize likely defense arguments (e.g., no part performance was rendered, no forfeiture clause exists, no adjustment made, no counter-claim exists). Cite ONLY provisions from the verified list.

must_include.description: At least one statutory section cited
Legal consequence explained (not just section named)

sections.heading: CAUSE OF ACTION
sections.instruction: Draft the cause of action section in PARAGRAPH prose (not numbered sub-headings). Must cover: (1) ORIGIN — when and how the right to sue first arose, anchored to the default/failure date, (2) FURTHER ACCRUAL — when the cause of action further arose (e.g., failure to comply with legal notice), (3) CONTINUITY — that the cause of action is continuing because the defendant continues to wrongfully retain the money. The cause must be anchored to the failure/default date, NOT the notice date. Write in flowing paragraph form — avoid mechanical sub-headings like '(a) underlying obligation'.

sections.heading: LIMITATION
sections.instruction: Draft the limitation section stating: (1) the applicable Article of the Limitation Act, 1963 Schedule (from enrichment or use placeholder), (2) the limitation period, (3) the starting date of limitation (must be anchored to cause_of_action_date/default_date, NOT notice_date), (4) that the suit is within time. If limitation article is not available in enrichment, use {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} placeholder.

sections.heading: VALUATION AND COURT FEE
sections.instruction: Draft the valuation and court fee section stating: (1) suit valuation equals the claim amount, (2) court fee calculated per applicable state court fees act. Use court_fee context from web search if available, otherwise use {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} placeholder.

sections.heading: INTEREST
sections.instruction: Draft the interest section with a 3-part structure: (1) Pre-suit interest — from default date to filing date with rate and justification, (2) Pendente lite interest — during pendency of suit under Section 34 CPC with rate, (3) Future interest — post-decree until realization with rate. Justify the interest rate: state why the claimed rate is appropriate (commercial transaction, wrongful retention, prevailing bank rate, contractual understanding). Cite Section 34 of CPC for pendente lite interest.

sections.heading: PRAYER
sections.instruction: Draft the prayer section with at least 6 specific sub-prayers as lettered items (a, b, c, ...). Each prayer must state exact amounts or rate placeholders — never vague. Must include: (a) decree for principal amount with Rs. figure, (b) pre-suit interest with rate and date range, (c) pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC with rate, (d) future interest post-decree with rate until realization, (e) costs of the suit including pre-litigation costs for sending legal notice, (f) any other relief the court deems fit and proper in the facts and circumstances.

sections.heading: LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON
sections.instruction: Generate a numbered list of documents the plaintiff relies upon, mapping to the annexure scheme. For each document: Serial No., Description, Annexure Label. Only list documents mentioned in evidence. If evidence is empty, list standard expected documents with {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}} placeholders.

sections.heading: VERIFICATION
sections.body:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this {{FILING_DATE}}.

As per Order VI Rule 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

The said cheque was returned unpaid by the drawee bank with the endorsement “Insufficient Funds” on {{DISHONOUR_DATE}}. The bank return memo/endorsement is produced as Annexure B.

4. Thereafter, the Plaintiff caused a statutory demand notice dated 01.02.2025 to be issued to the Defendant under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881 demanding payment of the cheque amount within 15 days from receipt of the notice. A copy of the notice is produced as Annexure C along with postal/courier receipts and tracking/acknowledgment as Annexure D.

5. The said notice was served on the Defendant on {{NOTICE_SERVICE_DATE}}. Despite service, the Defendant failed and neglected to pay the cheque amount within 15 days. The 15-day period expired on {{NOTICE_15_DAY_EXPIRY_DATE}}. No payment has been made till date.

6. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant’s conduct attracts the consequences contemplated under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881. The Plaintiff craves leave to pursue such criminal remedy as may be advised, and the present civil suit for recovery is without prejudice to such remedy.

7. Hence, the Plaintiff is constrained to file the present suit for recovery of Rs.10,00,000/- together with interest @ 18% per annum and costs.


LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS

1. The present suit is a civil suit for recovery of money arising from the Defendant’s legally enforceable debt/liability of Rs.10,00,000/- owed to the Plaintiff and the Defendant’s failure to honour payment.

2. The Defendant issued the cheque dated 15.01.2025 for Rs.10,00,000/- towards the said liability, and the cheque was dishonoured with the endorsement “Insufficient Funds”. These facts are pleaded to show the Defendant’s default and to evidence the Plaintiff’s bona fides; the present civil recovery suit is maintainable independently on the underlying liability and non-payment.

4. The Plaintiff claims interest at 18% per annum on account of the Defendant’s wrongful retention of the Plaintiff’s money and the commercial/mercantile nature of the transaction as set out in {{UNDERLYING_TRANSACTION_DETAILS}}.

5. The Plaintiff is entitled to pendente lite and future interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, and to costs of the suit.


CAUSE OF ACTION

The cause of action is continuing as the Defendant continues to default and withhold payment. The cause of action arose at {{CAUSE_OF_ACTION_PLACE}} and within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.


LIMITATION

The present suit is within limitation. The suit being a civil action for recovery of money based on the Defendant’s liability and non-payment is governed by the Limitation Act, 1963 and is required to be filed within the prescribed period of limitation applicable to such money claims.

The present suit filed on {{FILING_DATE}} is within three years thereof and is therefore within time.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

The suit is valued for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee at Rs.10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakh Only), being the principal amount claimed.

Court fee is payable as per the applicable State Court Fees Act on the above valuation. The Plaintiff pays/undertakes to pay court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} and craves leave to make good any deficit court fee, if any, as may be directed by this Hon’ble Court.


INTEREST

1. The Plaintiff claims pre-suit interest on Rs.10,00,000/- at 18% per annum from {{DISHONOUR_DATE}} (or such other date as this Hon’ble Court may determine as the date when the amount became due) till {{FILING_DATE}}.

2. The Plaintiff claims pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC at 18% per annum on Rs.10,00,000/- from {{FILING_DATE}} till the date of decree.

3. The Plaintiff claims future interest under Section 34 CPC at 18% per annum on the decretal amount from the date of decree till realization.


PRAYER

In view of the facts and circumstances stated above, the Plaintiff prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree directing the Defendant to pay to the Plaintiff a sum of Rs.10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakh Only) towards the cheque amount.

(b) Award pre-suit interest at 18% per annum on Rs.10,00,000/- from {{DISHONOUR_DATE}} till {{FILING_DATE}}.

(c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC at 18% per annum on Rs.10,00,000/- from {{FILING_DATE}} till the date of decree.

(d) Award future interest under Section 34 CPC at 18% per annum on the decretal amount from the date of decree till realization.

(e) Award costs of the suit, including notice charges and incidental expenses quantified at Rs. {{TOTAL_COSTS}}/- (including notice charges of Rs. {{LEGAL_NOTICE_COST}}/-).

(f) Grant such other and further reliefs as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.


LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON

1. Annexure A: Cheque bearing No. {{CHEQUE_NO}} dated 15.01.2025 for Rs.10,00,000/-.
2. Annexure B: Bank return memo/endorsement dated {{DISHONOUR_DATE}} showing “Insufficient Funds”.
3. Annexure C: Copy of statutory legal notice dated 01.02.2025 issued under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881.
4. Annexure D: Postal/courier receipt(s), tracking report(s), and acknowledgment/returned cover (as applicable) showing dispatch/service of notice.
5. Annexure E: Reply to notice (if any) received from the Defendant.
6. Annexure F: Any other supporting documents (if any) relevant to the underlying transaction/liability.


VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of the above plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and the contents based on legal advice are believed to be true on the basis of such advice. No part of this plaint is false and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

blocking_issues.issue: Civil plaint incorrectly treats Section 138 NI Act as the primary cause of action for a "suit under Section 138" and states "statutory preconditions for a suit under Section 138"; Section 138 is a criminal offence provision and does not itself create/define a civil recovery suit before the District Court.
blocking_issues.fix: Rewrite the Legal Basis/Grounds to state that the suit is a civil money recovery action based on an admitted/legally enforceable debt/liability and issuance of cheque as the instrument/acknowledgment; mention Section 138 NI Act only as background facts (dishonour + notice + non-payment) and clarify that criminal remedy is separate/without prejudice. Remove any language suggesting the present proceeding is a Section 138 "suit".
blocking_issues.location: LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS (entire section) and references in JURISDICTION / PRAYER implying a "suit under Section 138"

blocking_issues.issue: Facts section introduces fabricated/unsupported materials (e.g., "financial arrangement evidenced by Annexure A", loan agreement terms, advance payment date, contractual 18% interest under Annexure A, multiple annexures A–F including payment proof/acknowledgment documents) not provided in USER_REQUEST; this can be false pleading if filed as-is.
blocking_issues.fix: Rewrite FACTS as a chronological narrative strictly limited to provided facts (cheque issuance, presentation, dishonour endorsement, notice dated 01.02.2025, failure to pay within 15 days) and use {{PLACEHOLDER}} only for genuinely unknown particulars (cheque number, presentation date, dishonour date, notice service date, underlying transaction details). Update annexure/document references to match only those documents that necessarily exist from the stated facts.
blocking_issues.location: FACTS OF THE CASE, and consequential references to Annexures A–F throughout the draft

blocking_issues.issue: Limitation section cites and relies on Article 16 of the Limitation Act (goods sold & delivered to be paid by bill of exchange), which does not match the pleaded cheque-bounce money recovery factual matrix; limitation anchor is legally incorrect as drafted.
blocking_issues.fix: Replace Article 16 discussion with a limitation plea appropriate to a civil money recovery based on cheque/underlying liability, stating the suit is within the 3-year period computed from the date the amount became due / date of dishonour / date of demand (use {{DISHONOUR_DATE}} / {{DEMAND_DATE}} placeholders as needed) without citing an incorrect Article number.

blocking_issues.issue: Incorrect/irrelevant procedural-evidence references: draft states evidence will be admissible under "Rule 7 and 8 of the Evidence provisions governing Section 138"—this appears to confuse CPC evidence-recording provisions (Order XVIII) and is not a correct basis for admissibility in this plaint.
blocking_issues.fix: Delete the incorrect sentence(s) about Rule 7/8 and "Evidence under Section 138" and confine pleading to ordinary proof by documents/witnesses in a civil suit.
blocking_issues.location: LEGAL BASIS AND GROUNDS (sentence beginning "Evidence in support...")

blocking_issues.issue: Interest periods in Prayer are internally inconsistent/legally incorrect: pendente lite interest is claimed from 15.01.2025 (pre-suit) instead of from the date of institution; pre-suit interest start date is inconsistently tied to 01.02.2025 (notice date) without linking it to when the sum became due/dishonour.
blocking_issues.fix: Amend prayer to (i) claim pre-suit interest from {{DISHONOUR_DATE}} (or {{DATE_OF_DEMAND}}) to {{FILING_DATE}}, and (ii) claim pendente lite interest from {{FILING_DATE}} to date of decree under Section 34 CPC; keep future interest from decree till realization.
blocking_issues.location: PRAYER clauses (b) and (c); INTEREST section (for consistency)

blocking_issues.issue: Known core suit particulars (claim amount) are left as placeholders in the title/heading despite being provided (Rs.10,00,000/-), and the draft inconsistently uses 1,000,000 vs 10,00,000.
blocking_issues.fix: Populate claim amount and amount-in-words consistently as Rs.10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakh Only) across the plaint, including title, valuation, prayer, and narrative.
blocking_issues.location: Title/Heading, FACTS, VALUATION AND COURT FEE, INTEREST, PRAYER

non_blocking_issues.issue: Jurisdiction paragraph includes placeholders and an unnecessary speculative line "The agreement, if any, between the parties was executed at..."; territorial links should be stated on actual facts (defendant residence/business, plaintiff bank where cheque was presented, place of issuance/dishonour/notice service).
non_blocking_issues.fix: Replace speculative agreement execution sentence with concrete territorial facts and placeholders tied to cheque presentation/dishonour and defendant address (e.g., cheque presented through plaintiff’s bank at {{PLAINTIFF_BANK_BRANCH_PLACE}}; notice served at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}).

non_blocking_issues.issue: List of Documents numbering starts at 7 and uses repetitive {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}} placeholders, reducing readability.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Renumber list from 1 onward and replace {{DOCUMENT_DESCRIPTION}} with specific document names (cheque, return memo, notice, postal receipts) while retaining placeholders only where necessary.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Cause of action section uses multiple placeholders (e.g., {{DEFAULT_DATE}}) where the cheque date and notice date are known; this reduces precision.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Hard-code 15.01.2025 (cheque date) and 01.02.2025 (notice date) and keep placeholders only for unknown dates (presentation/dishonour/service/expiry).

unsupported_statements.statement: “The Plaintiff and the Defendant became involved in a financial arrangement evidenced by Annexure A, whereby the Plaintiff agreed to lend Rs 1,000,000 to the Defendant… under the terms set out in Annexure A…”
unsupported_statements.reason: USER_REQUEST does not mention any written loan agreement/Annexure A or agreed contractual interest terms; this appears invented.

unsupported_statements.statement: “The cheque dispute and its dishonour are evidenced by Annexure B, which includes the bank’s record of the dishonour and related details.” (as drafted with broad "related details" and later calling Annexure B as payment proof in the list)
unsupported_statements.reason: Only dishonour memo is supported by USER_REQUEST; "payment proof" and expansive supporting material is not provided and may not exist.
unsupported_statements.location: FACTS OF THE CASE, para 3; LIST OF DOCUMENTS (Annexure B description)

unsupported_statements.statement: “Annexure C and Annexure D document the notice and any potential response, respectively, though no payment or adequate reply was received.”
unsupported_statements.reason: USER_REQUEST states notice was sent and no payment within 15 days; it does not state any reply exists, nor that a reply was received/its contents.
unsupported_statements.location: FACTS OF THE CASE, para 5; LIST OF DOCUMENTS (Annexure D)

unsupported_statements.statement: “Annexure E for acknowledgment/admission documents (if any), and Annexure F for additional supporting documents.” (and reliance on them)
unsupported_statements.reason: No such acknowledgments/admissions/additional documents are provided in USER_REQUEST; pleading reliance may be misleading if they do not exist.
unsupported_statements.location: FACTS OF THE CASE, para 6; LIST OF DOCUMENTS (Annexure E/F)

unsupported_statements.statement: “Evidence… will be admissible under Rule 7 and 8 of the Evidence provisions governing Section 138…”
unsupported_statements.reason: Misstates the legal basis for admissibility and appears to confuse CPC evidence-recording provisions; not supported by RAG for this usage and not appropriate pleading for admissibility.

...Defendant

6. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant’s conduct attracts the consequences contemplated under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881. The Plaintiff craves leave to pursue such criminal remedy as may be advised, and the present civil suit for recovery is without prejudice to such remedy.

7. Hence, the Plaintiff is constrained to file the present suit for recovery of Rs.10,00,000/- together with interest @ 18% per annum and costs.

4. The Plaintiff claims interest at 18% per annum on account of the Defendant’s wrongful retention of the Plaintiff’s money and the commercial/mercantile nature of the transaction as set out in {{UNDERLYING_TRANSACTION_DETAILS}}.

5. The Plaintiff is entitled to pendente lite and future interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, and to costs of the suit.


CAUSE OF ACTION

The cause of action for the present suit first arose on {{DISHONOUR_DATE}} when the Defendant’s cheque bearing No. {{CHEQUE_NO}} dated 15.01.2025 for Rs.10,00,000/- drawn on State Bank of India, {{BANK_BRANCH}}, was returned unpaid with endorsement “Insufficient Funds”. LIST OF DOCUMENTS RELIED UPON
